name: Shadow Banning
description: Shadow banning, also known as stealth banning, ghost banning, or comment suppression, is the act of blocking a user or their content from an online community in such a way that the user is unaware that they have been banned. The user can continue to post content and participate in the community, but their contributions are invisible to other users. Proponents argue that shadow banning is a widespread practice employed by social media platforms, search engines, and online forums to suppress certain viewpoints or control narratives without explicitly banning users. They claim it is a form of censorship that allows platforms to maintain the appearance of neutrality while silently silencing dissenting voices. Evidence cited often includes anecdotal accounts of users experiencing reduced visibility, engagement metrics dropping inexplicably, and posts not appearing in searches or feeds despite the user believing they are posting normally. Some point to leaked internal documents or statements from former employees as further evidence. Mainstream perspective acknowledges that algorithmic content moderation exists and can unintentionally lead to reduced visibility for some users. Platforms argue that such occurrences are often due to algorithms designed to prioritize relevant content, combat spam, or enforce community guidelines, rather than intentional suppression of specific viewpoints. Critics also point out that perceived shadow banning can often be attributed to changes in platform algorithms, user error, or the user's content simply not being engaging or relevant to others. Independent verification of shadow banning claims is difficult due to the opaque nature of platform algorithms and the lack of transparency regarding content moderation practices.
subtitle: Stealth censorship in online communities

claims.claim: Social media platforms intentionally shadow ban users with dissenting viewpoints.
claims.evidence: Anecdotal accounts of reduced visibility, engagement metrics dropping, leaked internal documents.
claims.criticism: Platforms attribute reduced visibility to algorithmic content moderation, spam filters, and user error. Lack of independent verification.

claims.claim: Shadow banning is a form of censorship that allows platforms to control narratives without outright banning.
claims.evidence: Suppressed content may include political opinions, controversial topics, or criticism of the platform itself.
claims.criticism: Platforms claim to be enforcing community guidelines against hate speech, misinformation, or abusive behavior. Balancing free speech with platform responsibility.

claims.claim: Shadow banning is difficult to detect and prove due to the opaque nature of platform algorithms.
claims.evidence: Users may not realize they are shadow banned, making it hard to gather evidence.
claims.criticism: Platforms argue that transparency is limited to protect algorithms from manipulation and prevent abuse of the system.

keyFigures.name: Shiva Ayyadurai
keyFigures.role: Claims to have been shadow banned on Twitter and advocates against censorship.

keyFigures.name: Platforms' spokespeople
keyFigures.role: Defend content moderation policies and deny intentional shadow banning of specific viewpoints.

mythologyConnections: The concept of invisibility or being unseen, similar to cloaking devices in mythology.
The idea of silencing dissent, mirroring stories of forbidden knowledge or suppressed truths.

timeline.event: Early reports of forum administrators using stealth banning techniques.
Increased awareness and accusations of shadow banning on social media platforms.
Ongoing debate and controversy surrounding shadow banning and content moderation policies.

sources.author: O'Neil, Cathy
sources.work: Weapons of Math Destruction: How Big Data Increases Inequality and Threatens Democracy
sources.title: O'Neil, Cathy
